2604.27895.title: Graph World Models: Concepts, Taxonomy, and Future Directions
2604.27895.paper_summary: As one of the mainstream models of artificial intelligence, world models allow agents to learn the representation of the environment for efficient prediction and planning. However, classical world models based on flat tensors face several key problems, including noise sensitivity, error accumulation and weak reasoning. To address these limitations, many recent studies use graph structure to decompose the environment into entity nodes and interactive edges, and model virtual environments in a structured space. This paper systematically formalizes and unifies these emerging graph-based works under the concept of graph world models (GWMs). To the best of our knowledge, GWMs have not yet been explicitly defined and surveyed as a unified research paradigm. Furthermore, we propose a taxonomy based on relational inductive biases (RIB), categorizing GWMs by the specific structural priors they inject: (1) spatial RIB for topological abstraction; (2) physical RIB for dynamic simulation; and (3) logical RIB for causal and semantic reasoning. For each model category, we outline the key design principles, summarize representative models, and conduct comparative analyses. We further discuss open challenges and future directions, including dynamic graph adaptation, probabilistic relational dynamics, multi-granularity inductive biases, and the need for dedicated benchmarks and evaluation metrics for GWMs.
2604.27895.paper_summary_zh: 作為人工智慧的主流模型之一，世界模型允許代理學習環境的表示，以便進行有效的預測和規劃。<lb/>然而，基於平面張量的經典世界模型面臨幾個關鍵問題，包括噪音敏感性、錯誤累積和推理能力不足。<lb/>為了應對這些限制，許多最近的研究使用圖結構將環境分解為實體節點和互動邊，並在結構化空間中建模虛擬環境。<lb/>本文系統地形式化並統一這些新興的基於圖的研究，提出圖世界模型（GWMs）的概念。<lb/>據我們所知，GWMs尚未明確定義和調查作為一個統一的研究範式。<lb/>此外，我們提出了一個基於關係歸納偏見（RIB）的分類法，根據它們注入的特定結構先驗來對GWMs進行分類：<lb/>(1) 用於拓撲抽象的空間RIB；<lb/>(2) 用於動態模擬的物理RIB；<lb/>(3) 用於因果和語義推理的邏輯RIB。<lb/>對於每個模型類別，我們概述了關鍵設計原則，總結了代表性模型，並進行了比較分析。<lb/>我們進一步討論了開放挑戰和未來方向，包括動態圖適應、概率關係動態、多粒度歸納偏見，以及對GWMs專門基準和評估指標的需求。
2604.27895.author: Jiawei Liu et.al.
2604.27895.authors: Jiawei Liu, Senqiao Yang, Mingjun Wang, Yu Wang, Bei Yu
